description: Text is revised to clearly state that Bourgain-Kontorovich's 2δ−1 exponent controls the growth of R(d)—the count of d representable as n/d—but does not provide an effective or quantitative rate for density convergence itself. States explicitly that BK proves density 1 non-effectively, and 'logarithmic' behavior observed here is not theoretically predicted by BK.
